title: Protect Funding for Women's Health Care Act
short_title: Protect Funding for Women's Health Care Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
plain_summary: This bill stops the federal government from giving money to Planned Parenthood and any organizations connected to it. Instead, the money would go to other health care providers that help women, like community health centers and hospitals. The goal is to make sure women can still get health care services, but not through Planned Parenthood. The bill would cut off all federal funding that currently goes to Planned Parenthood clinics across the country.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
subjects: Social work, volunteer service, charitable organizations

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protect Funding for Women's Health Care Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. First, many different places already provide health services to women and will keep doing so. These places include state and county health departments, community health centers, hospitals, doctor offices, and other organizations. They provide services like lab tests, x-rays, well-child checkups, care before and after having babies, shots to prevent disease, family planning services including birth control, tests for sexually transmitted diseases, cervical and breast cancer screenings, and referrals to other doctors. Second, many of these places serve everyone regardless of whether they can afford to pay. They also provide services in areas where medical care is hard to find and to people who have trouble getting medical care. Third, all money that will no longer go to Planned Parenthood Federation of America will still be available for other qualified organizations to provide women's health care services.

sections.title: Section 3 Prohibition
sections.text: No matter what any other law says, no federal money can be given to Planned Parenthood Federation of America or to any of its related organizations, branch offices, replacement organizations, or clinics. However, this law does not change any limits on abortion funding that are already in spending laws passed by Congress. This law also does not reduce the total amount of federal money available to support women's health care.
